alerts.reason: location L-10 limit 8,500,000 vs single-location max 10,000,000

disclaimer: Monitoring alert only; exposure and modeled-loss figures are estimates for human review. No underwriting, reinsurance, capacity, reserving, or system-of-record action has been taken.
narrative: Scheduled catastrophe-exposure run cem-2026-07-18-0007 over Tropical Storm Echo (AL092026, cat 1) fired 1 early-warning alert in the Houston footprint under the deployment's non-default approaching_ratio of 0.8. Location L-10 single-location limit (8,500,000) is Informational at exceedance ratio 0.85 against the 10,000,000 single-location max; because approaching_ratio is 0.8 for this pack, an 0.85 ratio bands as Informational (early warning), not below-threshold. All sources are fresh so confidence is normal and no data gaps were found. This is an estimate queued to catastrophe-risk-review for a human; the catastrophe-risk / portfolio manager decides any response. Monitoring alert only; exposure and modeled-loss figures are estimates for human review. No underwriting, reinsurance, capacity, reserving, or system-of-record action has been taken.
notes: Early-warning-only run: the sole breach is an approaching-band single-location alert surfaced because this deployment lowers approaching_ratio to 0.8; nothing is at or over a hard threshold.
